text: ReMEM is a lightweight, framework-agnostic memory substrate for AI agents that provides a persistent memory layer capable of storing content, querying by meaning, organizing across layers, and surviving restarts.

evidence.quote: ReMEM is a lightweight, framework-agnostic memory substrate for AI agents.

text: ReMEM's core insight is that prompts should be external environment variables, not direct context, applied to the problem of persistent, queryable agent memory.

evidence.quote: It applies the core insight from Recursive Language Models (RLMs) - that prompts should be external environment variables, not direct context - to the problem of persistent, queryable agent memory.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: LLMs are limited by their context window.

evidence.quote: LLMs are limited by their context window.

text: The RLM-Style Memory REPL enables a model to navigate memory programmatically by writing JavaScript, never holding all memory in context—only constant-size metadata—thus allowing arbitrarily large memory stores without context window overflow.

evidence.quote: The model writes JavaScript to navigate memory.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: ReMEM maintains four weighted retrieval layers: Episodic (1 hour TTL, weight 0.2), Semantic (7 days TTL, weight 0.3), Identity (30 days TTL, weight 0.5), and Procedural (30 days TTL, weight 0.4).

evidence.quote:
| Episodic | 1 hour | 0.2 | Raw recent interactions |
| Semantic | 7 days | 0.3 | Synthesized facts, preferences, decisions |
| Identity | 30 days | 0.5 | Core identity signals and values |
| Procedural | 30 days | 0.4 | Learned behaviors and triggered rules |

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p120.s001`..`p123.s006` around the ref
5 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Hybrid scoring combines 40% keyword relevance with 60% cosine similarity when vector embeddings are available; when embeddings are unavailable, it falls back to keyword plus access count scoring.

evidence.quote: Hybrid scoring (v0.4.1): When EmbeddingService is wired into LayerManager, the content relevance score is a hybrid: 40% keyword matching + 60% cosine similarity.

text: Temporal validity enforces that semantic layer entries with validUntil timestamps older than the current time are automatically filtered out from all layer queries.

evidence.quote: Temporal validity is enforced in all layer queries — entries with validUntil < now are automatically filtered out and not returned.

text: Episodic compression automatically compresses old episodic entries into LLM-generated semantic summaries (2-4 sentences) when the episodic layer exceeds 80% capacity, preserving meaning while reclaiming storage.

evidence.quote: When the episodic layer fills above 80% capacity, old entries are LLM-compressed into semantic summaries instead of lost to TTL eviction.

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Memory consolidation deduplicates entries via embedding or keyword similarity, resolves conflicts by detecting contradictions and marking older entries as superseded, and promotes frequently-accessed episodic entries to the semantic layer.

evidence.quote: Memory consolidation (v0.6.1) - Cross-layer deduplication via embedding/keyword similarity, conflict resolution with contradiction detection, cross-layer promotion of frequently-accessed episodic entries to semantic layer

text: Memory links create explicit typed relationships between memories (about, supports, contradicts, etc.) enabling weighted graph-adjacent recall and optional traversal path details.

evidence.quote: Memory links + neighbor-aware retrieval (v0.8.0, expanded in v0.8.5) - Explicit typed links between memories (about, supports, contradicts, etc.), weighted graph-adjacent recall, and optional traversal path details

text: Identity alignment auditing detects drift from a constitution and provides corrective injection text to keep agent behavior anchored to stated values.

evidence.quote: Identity alignment audits (v0.8.5) - Drift scoring plus corrective injection text for agents that need to keep behavior anchored to a constitution

text: Identity duplication backs up an agent's full identity to DARKSOL's server, while infection pulls and overlays an identity package on any ReMEM-equipped agent, requiring a live connection to persist.

evidence.quote: Persistence: Live connection required — cut the connection = overlay stops

text: Framework adapters (v0.6.1, expanded in v0.8.5) - Dependency-free helpers for Vercel AI SDK, LangGraph-style stores, and OpenClaw/session memory with decision/procedure/project-context helpers

evidence.quote: Framework adapters (v0.6.1, expanded in v0.8.5) - Dependency-free helpers for Vercel AI SDK, LangGraph-style stores, and OpenClaw/session memory with decision/procedure/project-context helpers

text: Benchmark results show ReMEM achieved 99.4% recall@1 and 100% recall@5 on exact-codename lookups across 50,000 memories (3.6M tokens) with 49.98ms average query latency, compared to 0% recall for fixed recent-context baseline.

evidence.quote:
50,000 memories
Approx 3,625,526 stored tokens
Simulated active context: 7,264 tokens
Corpus/window pressure: 499x
Fixed recent-context recall: 0%
ReMEM exact-codename lookup: 99.4% recall@1, 100% recall@5
Avg query latency: 49.98ms local in-memory sql.js run

evidence.quote: SQLite via sql.js - WebAssembly-compiled SQLite. No native binaries. Cross-platform by default.

evidence.quote: Plug-and-play LLM abstraction - Bankr, OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama - swap without changing your code

text: Small embedding-backed semantic retrieval achieved 100% recall@1 and 100% recall@5 on 80 memories, while identifying embedding ingestion as the current performance bottleneck.

evidence.quote: Small embedding-backed semantic run: 100% recall@1/@5 on 80 memories, with embedding ingestion identified as the current bottleneck

text: PostgreSQL vector search currently computes cosine similarity in application memory rather than using native pgvector, which pgvector indexing will address in a future optimization.

evidence.quote: PostgreSQL vector search is brute-force for now - embeddings are stored in Postgres, but semantic search currently computes cosine similarity in application memory.

text: Drift detection pattern-matching is fragile - Only fires on specific negation patterns (prefer not, no longer, changed to, etc.). LLM fallback requires a separate eval model.

evidence.quote: Drift detection pattern-matching is fragile - Only fires on specific negation patterns (prefer not, no longer, changed to, etc.). LLM fallback requires a separate eval model.

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p232.s004`..`p232.s006` around the ref

text: The episodic layer has a short 1-hour TTL that may need tuning for long-running automation agents.

evidence.quote: Episodic layer TTL is short (1h) - May need tuning for long-running automation agents.

text: The Episodic Capture Pipeline buffers events with importance scoring (type-based base scores), deduplicates rapid identical events, extracts topics from event content and hashtags, and batch-writes to the MemoryStore.

evidence.quote: Episodic capture pipeline (v0.5.0) - Automatic event capture for the episodic layer.

text: Snapshot/restore provides full core and layered memory snapshots with SHA-256 integrity checks and portable export/import for long-running agents to survive restarts, migrations, and crashes.

evidence.quote: Snapshot/restore (v0.6.2) - Full core + layered memory snapshots with SHA-256 integrity checks and portable export/import for long-running agents.

text: Do not claim ReMEM universally extends every model's context window, 100% semantic recall at large scale, production latency from synthetic in-memory sql.js results, or embedding ingestion performance independent of hardware and model.

evidence.quote:
Do not claim:
ReMEM universally extends every model's context window.
100% semantic recall at large scale.
Production latency from synthetic in-memory sql.js results.
Embedding ingestion performance independent of hardware/model.
